findings.title: GET /projects/:projectId/clients — missing project-scope access check (IDOR)
findings.description: The listProjectClients method, called by GET /projects/:projectId/clients in ProjectsClientsController, performs no project ownership or membership check before returning clients attached to a project. Any authenticated user holding the 'clients:read' permission can enumerate the client organisations associated with any project, regardless of whether they are a member of that project. The rest of the codebase (documents, tasks, time-tracking) consistently calls accessScope.assertCanAccessProject() before returning project-scoped data; this endpoint skips that pattern entirely.
findings.root_cause: listProjectClients never calls assertCanAccessProject(), unlike every other project-scoped read endpoint in the codebase.
findings.impact: Authenticated users with clients:read can enumerate client names (organisation names, active status) attached to any project — including projects they are not members of and that may be confidential.
findings.suggested_fix: Inject AccessScopeService into ClientsService and call assertCanAccessProject(projectId, currentUser) at the start of listProjectClients. Thread the currentUser from ProjectsClientsController.list() through the service call, matching the pattern used in DocumentsService.create().
acceptance_criteria: A user without project membership receives 403 on GET /projects/:otherProjectId/clients
A user with projects:manage_any still receives the list (bypass preserved)
Existing members receive the list unchanged
findings.notes: Pattern gap confirmed by cross-referencing assertCanAccessProject usage across the codebase.

findings.title: GET /projects/:projectId/third-party-members — missing project-scope access check (IDOR)
findings.description: listProjectMembers, called by GET /projects/:projectId/third-party-members in ProjectsThirdPartyMembersController, returns all third-party organisations (contractors, legal entities) attached to any project without checking project membership. A user with third_parties:read can iterate any projectId to discover which external partners are involved in projects they have no business seeing.
findings.root_cause: listProjectMembers never calls assertCanAccessProject(), unlike task, document, and time-tracking equivalents.
findings.impact: Disclosure of sensitive contractor/partner relationships to users outside the project, potentially revealing procurement or contractual information.
findings.suggested_fix: Inject AccessScopeService into ThirdPartiesService and call assertCanAccessProject(projectId, currentUser) at the start of listProjectMembers. Pass currentUser from the controller.
acceptance_criteria: Non-member with third_parties:read receives 403 on GET /projects/:otherProjectId/third-party-members
Project members receive the list unchanged
Users with projects:manage_any bypass the check
findings.notes: Same pattern gap as security-S4-1 in the sibling module.

findings.title: GET /tasks/:taskId/third-party-assignees — missing task-scope access check (IDOR)
findings.description: listTaskAssignees, called by GET /tasks/:taskId/third-party-assignees in TasksThirdPartyAssigneesController, returns all third parties assigned to any task without checking task or project access for the requesting user. Any user with third_parties:read can enumerate third-party assignees for any task, including tasks in confidential projects.
findings.root_cause: listTaskAssignees never calls assertCanReadTask() or assertCanAccessProject(), unlike the tasks module which consistently enforces scope.
findings.impact: Disclosure of contractor/partner identities on tasks the caller cannot read, potentially leaking confidential project personnel information.
findings.suggested_fix: Call accessScope.assertCanReadTask(taskId, currentUser) at the top of listTaskAssignees. Inject AccessScopeService and thread currentUser from the controller.
acceptance_criteria: Assigned task members receive the list unchanged
Users with tasks:manage_any bypass the check
findings.notes: Confidential tasks (confidential: true) are specifically protected in AccessScopeService.taskReadWhere; this gap bypasses that protection.

findings.title: CreateDocumentDto.size missing @IsInt() and @Min(0) — negative or non-integer values accepted
findings.description: The size field is declared as number with only @IsNotEmpty(). No @IsInt(), @IsNumber(), or @Min(0) decorator is present. A client can submit size: -1 or size: 0 which will pass DTO validation. The service only rejects values > 200_000_000. The DB column is Int (Prisma), but negative values are valid PostgreSQL integers. The self-reported nature of the field means this is primarily a data-quality issue, but it allows storage of semantically invalid metadata (negative file sizes).
findings.root_cause: Incomplete validator coverage on the size field in CreateDocumentDto.
findings.impact: Negative or non-integer size values are persisted to the database, corrupting document metadata. Analytics or reporting based on size aggregation could produce incorrect results.
findings.suggested_fix: Add @IsInt() @Min(0) to the size field. Also add @Max(MAX_DOCUMENT_SIZE_BYTES) to move the cap from the service layer to the DTO, making the constraint self-documenting.
acceptance_criteria: POST /documents with size: 0 is accepted (or rejected if 0-byte files should be disallowed)
POST /documents with size: 999999999999 returns 400 if @Max is added
findings.notes: UpdateDocumentDto inherits this gap via PartialType(CreateDocumentDto).

findings.title: CreateDocumentDto.description missing @MaxLength — unbounded free-text field
findings.description: The description field has @IsString() and @IsOptional() but no @MaxLength constraint. A client can supply a very long description string. The 1MB global body limit in main.ts provides a hard ceiling, but best practice (and the existing pattern in this same DTO: name has @MaxLength(255), url has @MaxLength(2048)) is to explicitly cap free-text fields. This also documents intent in the API contract.
findings.root_cause: MaxLength decorator omitted from an optional free-text field.
findings.impact: Near-megabyte description strings can be persisted; minor storage waste and potential UI rendering issues on the frontend.
findings.suggested_fix: Add @MaxLength(2000) or a similar business-appropriate limit to description, consistent with the notes field cap used in ThirdParty.
acceptance_criteria: POST /documents with description longer than the cap returns 400
POST /documents with a short description is accepted
findings.notes: Partially mitigated by the 1MB Fastify body limit.

findings.title: QueryClientsDto.search and QueryThirdPartyDto.search missing @MaxLength — unbounded query string parameter
findings.description: The search parameter in both QueryClientsDto (clients) and QueryThirdPartyDto (third-parties) has no @MaxLength. The value is passed to Prisma as a LIKE pattern (mode: insensitive), which is parameterized (no injection risk), but an arbitrarily long search string could stress the DB full-scan ILIKE query. The GET body limit does not apply to query string parameters; Fastify/nginx URL limits are the only cap (~8KB by default).
findings.root_cause: MaxLength omitted from search fields in both query DTOs.
findings.impact: A very long search string (e.g. 8KB of pattern) causes a PostgreSQL ILIKE on the organizationName/name column with a huge pattern, slightly degrading performance. Low impact given DB size constraints.
findings.suggested_fix: Add @MaxLength(200) or @MaxLength(255) to the search fields in both QueryClientsDto and QueryThirdPartyDto.
findings.notes: Same pattern in both DTOs. Third-parties has the same omission at line 31-33.

findings.title: CreateThirdPartyDto.contactEmail missing @MaxLength
findings.description: contactEmail is validated with @IsEmail() which enforces valid email syntax but imposes no explicit byte-length cap. RFC 5321 limits email addresses to 254 characters; class-validator's @IsEmail() may or may not enforce this internally depending on version. The Prisma schema stores this as String? (TEXT in PostgreSQL) with no DB-level length constraint. Explicit @MaxLength(254) documents intent and adds a defence-in-depth layer.
findings.root_cause: @MaxLength not added alongside @IsEmail() on the contactEmail field.
findings.impact: Oversized email strings (>254 chars) may be stored, which cannot be valid email addresses. Downstream display/email-sending code may behave unexpectedly.
findings.suggested_fix: Add @MaxLength(254) to contactEmail field, consistent with RFC 5321 limits.
acceptance_criteria: POST /third-parties with contactEmail of 300 characters returns 400
POST /third-parties with a valid email up to 254 chars is accepted
findings.notes: UpdateThirdPartyDto inherits this gap via PartialType(CreateThirdPartyDto).

verified_clean: apps/api/src/clients/clients.controller.ts — all routes have @RequirePermissions, ParseUUIDPipe on params, no raw SQL
apps/api/src/clients/clients.service.ts — all Prisma queries parameterized, no $queryRawUnsafe, hardcoded secrets absent
apps/api/src/clients/dto/create-client.dto.ts — name has @Length(1,255), @IsString, @Transform trim
apps/api/src/clients/dto/update-client.dto.ts — optional fields properly validated, @IsBoolean on isActive
apps/api/src/clients/projects-clients.controller.ts — @RequirePermissions on all routes, ParseUUIDPipe on all params
apps/api/src/documents/documents.controller.ts — @RequirePermissions on all routes, OwnershipGuard on PATCH/DELETE, ParseUUIDPipe on id params
apps/api/src/documents/documents.service.ts — Prisma queries parameterized, no raw SQL, audit trail fires after access check
apps/api/src/documents/dto/create-document.dto.ts — @IsUrl with protocol allowlist (http/https), @IsIn on mimeType (SVG excluded), @MaxLength(255) on name, @MaxLength(2048) on url
apps/api/src/documents/dto/update-document.dto.ts — inherits CreateDocumentDto validators via PartialType
apps/api/src/third-parties/third-parties.controller.ts — all routes have @RequirePermissions, ParseUUIDPipe on params
apps/api/src/third-parties/third-parties.service.ts — Prisma queries parameterized, no raw SQL, no hardcoded secrets
apps/api/src/third-parties/dto/create-third-party.dto.ts — @IsEnum on type, @MaxLength on all string fields except contactEmail, @IsEmail on contactEmail
apps/api/src/third-parties/dto/update-third-party.dto.ts — PartialType inheritance, @IsBoolean on isActive
apps/api/src/third-parties/dto/assign-third-party-to-project.dto.ts — @IsUUID, @IsInt/@Min/@Max on allocation
apps/api/src/third-parties/dto/assign-third-party-to-task.dto.ts — @IsUUID, @IsNotEmpty
apps/api/src/third-parties/projects-third-party-members.controller.ts — @RequirePermissions on all routes, ParseUUIDPipe on params
apps/api/src/third-parties/tasks-third-party-assignees.controller.ts — @RequirePermissions on all routes, ParseUUIDPipe on params
Global: ValidationPipe configured with whitelist: true, forbidNonWhitelisted: true, transform: true
Global: APP_GUARD enforces RBAC, RBAC_GUARD_MODE=permissive blocked in production
Global: 1MB body limit on Fastify adapter
No $queryRawUnsafe or $executeRawUnsafe in any file in scope
No hardcoded secrets, tokens, or passwords found in any file in scope
Document URL not fetched server-side — no SSRF risk; URL is stored metadata only
